_readme: Plan-map data. One entry per topic. Ten-second capture: append {id,title,campaign,status} — wire deps/links later. Viewer: /Aralia/planmap/index.html on any dev server. status: parked | specced | active | done. deps: ids this topic REQUIRES. A dep may also carry `feature: <slug>` to narrow the arrow to ONE feature of the target topic (slug = that feature's title run through the same slugifier as the ids); the `why` still explains the edge, the pointer is now structural. link: repo-relative spec doc path (opened via the dev server's /@fs bridge using repoRoot). Truth-ownership + promotion rules (who owns statuses/decisions/deps; gaps flow UP by human curation only, plan flows DOWN via planmap-to-wave): tools/agora/PLANNING-STACK.md. `open`: integer mirror of the linked subspec's '## Open' bullet count (recompute when the doc changes — it is NOT invented, it is counted). `spike: true`: research that could invalidate the approach (dashed ⚠ tile). status also allows 'superseded' (dead decision — drops to the Graveyard strip; add a one-line `killed` reason). `focus: true` on at most ONE topic = what Remy is actually touching now (★ FOCUS).

topics.title: Beautification wave
topics.sub: beauty first, perf later · anchor: BG3 · realism climbs everywhere

deps.why: CHOSEN, mixed-grain: only the TSL code lifts (trees/grass/wind/clouds/sky/GI from fable5-world-demo, MIT) hard-require the new renderer — props/catalogs are renderer-agnostic — but building every visual packet once, on the final renderer, beats building twice

topics.title: Building blueprint pipeline
topics.sub: blueprint-first: design the 2D blueprint, build 3D from ONE shared data shape (rooms/doors/walls/stairs in feet)

deps.why: Grows the L4 interior generator into the shared building-maker rather than forking a second one: it already emits pure InteriorPlan data (rooms/doorways/furnishings/stairs, feet-canon 5 ft) and feeds the 3D build — the pipeline extends that data shape. Reverse experiment 2026-07-05 (PreviewBlueprint) proved one shared renderer serves the 2D blueprint AND the 3D build both ways

Interior furniture IS props: beds, tables, hearths, counters, barrels place through the prop schema + placement engine (with referee data) — same system dungeon dressing uses, no prop system no furnishing
Walls carry real thickness on the grid line so combat reads cover: the blueprint's wall model feeds the fight-in-place 5 ft tile referee — a building interior IS a combat surface

features.title: Blueprint data contract (rooms/doors/windows/furniture/stairs/walls, feet)
Believable layout (room purposes, dominant main room, real corridors, sensible entrance)
Module-map 2D blueprint drawer (pure view; scale bar + room numbers)
Walls as real-thickness grid edges (cover for the referee)
Basements + multi-storey
Irregular footprints (L-shapes, wings, tower) raised in 3D

topics.title: Procedural dungeon generator
topics.sub: CRITIQUE WAVE FOLDED IN 2026-07-06: 5 Opus critics filed 50 points (.agent/scratch/dungeon-critique.md); high-impact fixes landed same day — masonry wall bands, fixed+contrasted doors, secret doors + traps (new contract types), sparse depth-ordered keying, type-tinted rooms, boss-arena selection, DPR-crisp canvas, ?dseed= reproducible preview, RNG index guards, dead delaunay field removed; 10/10 tests green; remaining criticisms triaged for later (rotation/organic silhouette, sheet identity/title band, theming spine, verticality, feet-canon)

deps.why: Mirrors the L4 interior generator's contract 1:1 — deterministic seed paths, pure-data POJO output, zero THREE imports, feet-canon 5 ft grid; interiors proved the pattern this reuses
Dungeon decoration IS props: pillars, torches, chests, braziers and set-dressing place through the prop schema + placement engine (with referee data) — no prop system, no dressing

Dungeon rooms are combat encounters: fights resolve through the fight-in-place 5 ft tile referee, and difficulty maps to a real D&D XP budget — the dungeon is the purpose-built home for in-place combat

features.title: Pure-data generator (growth layout: door → corridor → room, loop doors through wall gaps)
Acceptance test suite (determinism, 100% connectivity, boss depth, loops = cyclomatic, prop/spawn legality, perf)
2D top-down Dungeon preview step with overlay toggles (Delaunay/MST/loops/critical/heatmap)
Feet-canon boundary conversion (rooms/props/spawns to feet per DungeonPlan contract)
WebGPU/TSL 3D scene builder (buildDungeonScene: instanced geometry + baked lighting)
Decoration richness + real monster/CR sourcing from the bestiary
World attachment point (hidden-place leaf / building stair-down / travel destination)

topics.title: WebGPURenderer migration
topics.sub: three.js WebGPU + TSL; unlocks MIT code lifts from fable5-world-demo; VALIDATED on the RTX 2070S (37fps headless, 15.5M tris)

features.title: WebGPU probe: streamed ground scene on WebGPURenderer
Material parity pass (terrain, water, buildings)
features.note: Root cause: R3F-mounted scene lights don't drive the WebGPU LightsNode (three #30044 / r3f #2853) -> zero irradiance -> black terrain. Fixed by baking hemisphere+sun Lambert into unlit MeshBasicNodeMaterial colorNodes (no scene lights). Verified on WebGL2-fallback node path; real-GPU eyeball still pending. Sky = fog+CSS backdrop (fine); postprocessing = backlog.

features.title: Probe full-stack parity
features.note: WHY (dep on the material parity pass): parity was proven only on flat terrain + building boxes while the ground world now ships trees, grass, the full prop set, styled roofs, walls/roads/decks. The probe (?phase=webgpuprobe) now renders the SAME full stack the live scene does at the same seed/pose, reusing the game's data (chunk bundles + GroundWorld.props) with the baked-TSL Lambert path. FAIL-FAST aligned with the battle map: pre-checks navigator.gpu + requestAdapter() before mounting; no adapter OR a non-WebGPU backend -> NO scene, an error pane ('WebGPU unavailable: <reason>') with one explicit 'View this scene in WebGL instead' button (routes to ?phase=world3d&ground=1 at the same pose; user-driven, never auto-fallback). Green 'WebGPU' + live FPS badge only on genuine success. Documented MISSING (WebGL-only, shown on-screen): real-time shadows on the node path (no LightsNode consumes a shadow map when lighting is baked into colorNode). Key node-path gotcha found + fixed: on InstancedMesh the InstanceNode multiplies instanceColor into the color pipeline UNCONDITIONALLY, so a dark per-instance scatter tint crushed lit trees/bushes to black — proven in isolation on a real WebGPU backend ([0,19,0] with dark instanceColor vs [109,158,88] without) and fixed by leaving instanceColor null (trees keep geometry leaf-green; bushes use a solid green). Headless capture correctly shows the error pane (no WebGPU adapter in headless Chromium); rendered WebGPU full-stack needs the real-GPU eyeball (RTX 2070S).

features.title: Battle-map + modal scenes migration
features.note: Battle-map WebGPU render path behind ?gpu=1 (WebGL default unchanged): BattleMap3D delegates to BattleMap3DGpuScene, same shared combat hooks/data, baked-TSL Lambert (no scene lights). FAIL-FAST preserved: probes navigator.gpu.requestAdapter() before mounting; no adapter -> NO scene, error panel + one explicit 'Use WebGL instead' button (user-driven, never auto-fallback); gl factory throws on a non-WebGPU backend; WebGPU badge only on genuine success. VISUAL PARITY PORT DONE (2026-07-05, rungs 1-4 + rung 5 wired): (1) procedural terrain texturing TRANSLATED from the onBeforeCompile GLSL to a TSL node graph (gpu/terrainColorNode.ts: per-type palettes, FBM/voronoi noise, organic edge blend, slope-rock, wet banks, dapple) — not a flat palette; (2) instanced grass + ground scatter (mirrors GrassLayer/GroundScatter); (3) grid + movement/path/AoE overlay translated to TSL (gpu/gridOverlayNodes.ts), fade-lerped, terrain-conforming, BattleMap3D passes validMoves/activePath/actionMode/aoeSet; (4) tokens gained team color + HP fade + selection/active rings; (5) node PostProcessing bloom+vignette wired (dynamic-imported, real-GPU-gated). Node graphs unit-tested WITHOUT a GPU (5 new tests); battle-map suites 12/8 green; tsc clean. STILL MISSING (WebGL-only, shown on-screen in a red list, no faking): real-time shadows (baked colorNode has no LightsNode for a shadow map), full animated CharacterActor rig + drei nameplates (1,491-line drei/AnimationMixer stack), GPU wind sway on grass. Captures: bm3d-port-webgl-ref.png (target look) + bm3d-port-webgpu.png (headless fail-fast pane, correct with no adapter). Rendered WebGPU battlefield still needs the real-GPU eyeball via ?gpu=1 (RTX 2070S). Modal (non-battle) scenes still parked.

topics.title: Agent retrace
topics.sub: recover a dead worker's WORK, not just reopen its task: preserve-on-reap dossier (identity + files held + say-tail + checkpoint) + `retrace <id>` shows the partial git diff; the successor reads it before continuing, never blind-restarts; reapCount escalates cursed tasks to the master. Specced 2026-07-06; not built

deps.why: The dossier is only useful if it captures WHO died and where from — retrace stamps the dead agent’s full identity record, so a bare handle is not enough; identity is the prerequisite

features.title: Preserve-on-reap: build the retrace record in sweepExpired before freeing locks + dropping the agent
task checkpoint --did --next --files (latest-wins resumable note) + reapCount escalation
retrace <taskId> command: dossier + live git diff of files held + successor resume protocol
